text: Here's what science actually says about old friends hypothesis. The old friends hypothesis suggests that familiar microbes help our immune system develop better. Without exposure to these microbes, our immune system might struggle, leading to allergies and autoimmune diseases. Research shows that kids in rural areas have fewer allergies, possibly from greater microbial diversity. So, reconnecting with old friends might not just feel good, it could also be vital for our health. Curious about how your environment shapes your health? Follow for more insights!
